modules.label: Skill IR
modules.objective: Platform-neutral capability contract exists before platform-specific packaging.
modules.next_action: Keep all target packages compiled from IR rather than hand-maintained per target.

modules.label: Output Eval Lab
modules.objective: with-skill/baseline output quality is measured with assertions, execution evidence, and blind review packs.
modules.command: python3 scripts/yao.py output-exec . && python3 scripts/yao.py output-review .
modules.next_action: Add more real-file and adversarial holdout cases as adoption data grows.

modules.label: Runtime Conformance
modules.objective: Target packages can be consumed by OpenAI, Claude, Agent Skills, VS Code, and generic targets.
modules.next_action: Keep conformance cases aligned with current platform metadata rules.

modules.label: Trust Security
modules.objective: Scripts, dependencies, permissions, secrets, and package hash are reviewable for team distribution.
modules.next_action: Keep high-permission approvals scoped, expiring, and mapped to target enforcement.

modules.label: Skill Atlas
modules.objective: Team skill portfolio reveals route collisions, stale ownership, dependency graph, and no-route opportunities.
modules.next_action: Use real telemetry to rank stale or drifting skills by impact, not only by static metadata.

modules.label: Registry Distribution
modules.objective: Skill packages are installable, versioned, checksumed, and upgrade-reviewable.
modules.command: python3 scripts/yao.py package . --platform openai --platform claude --platform generic --platform vscode --output-dir dist --zip && python3 scripts/yao.py registry-audit .
modules.next_action: Regenerate registry metadata after package verification so source and archive checksums stay aligned.

modules.label: Review Studio
modules.objective: One HTML page supports first-pass production review across trigger, output, runtime, trust, release, and evidence actions.
modules.next_action: Close pending human and external evidence before claiming full release readiness.

modules.label: Telemetry Drift
modules.objective: Real usage feedback is captured as metadata-only local-first drift signals.
modules.command: python3 scripts/yao.py telemetry-hooks . && python3 scripts/yao.py adoption-drift .
modules.next_action: Install a real client and import production metadata-only events into the local drift loop.

reference_extension_tracks.label: Skill Interpretation Report
reference_extension_tracks.objective: User-facing deep interpretation report explains use cases, triggers, inputs, outputs, workflow, principles, boundaries, quality gates, examples, and next iterations.
reference_extension_tracks.current: Skill Overview v2 is canonical and mirrored as first-class skill-interpretation HTML/JSON with schema and tests.
reference_extension_tracks.target: Either keep skill-overview as the canonical interpretation report with an explicit contract, or split a dedicated reports/skill-interpretation.* renderer and tests.
reference_extension_tracks.next_action: Keep overview and interpretation contracts in lockstep when report sections, metrics, or layout semantics change.

reference_extension_tracks.label: Adaptive Self-Iteration
reference_extension_tracks.objective: Local-first preference memory, repeated-signal extraction, adaptation proposals, approval, patch application, regression evidence, and rollback.
reference_extension_tracks.current: Full adaptive loop includes proposal, approval, patch application, regression evidence, and rollback metadata.
reference_extension_tracks.target: Proposal-only adaptation with explicit input source, redaction, allowlisted write targets, approval ledger, regression report, and rollback plan.
reference_extension_tracks.next_action: Start with policy and read-only scan tests; do not read shell history or private logs unless the user provides an explicit source path.

reference_extension_tracks.label: Daily SkillOps Report
reference_extension_tracks.objective: Daily operations layer summarizes explicit-source conversation patterns, proposal-only adaptation work, approval state, release locks, and world-class evidence gaps.
reference_extension_tracks.current: Daily SkillOps report is a CLI-backed, explicit-source operations cockpit with tests and committed dated evidence.
reference_extension_tracks.target: Generate reports/skillops/daily/YYYY-MM-DD.* from explicit sources without private log scanning, source writes, auto-patching, or world-class overclaiming.
reference_extension_tracks.next_action: Keep Daily SkillOps report aligned with proposal, approval, coverage, and world-class ledger contracts as the operations layer evolves.

reference_extension_tracks.label: Weekly Curator Report
reference_extension_tracks.objective: Weekly curator layer aggregates Daily SkillOps opportunities, Skill Atlas portfolio signals, release locks, and world-class evidence gaps into a maintenance queue.
reference_extension_tracks.current: Weekly curator report is CLI-backed, proposal-only, and backed by committed weekly JSON/Markdown evidence.
reference_extension_tracks.target: Generate reports/skillops/weekly/YYYY-WNN.* without private log scanning, source writes, auto-patching, or world-class overclaiming.
reference_extension_tracks.next_action: Use weekly curator output as the Skill Librarian maintenance queue before approving any durable skill-library changes.

next_highest_leverage: Close the four world-class evidence ledger entries with accepted human or external evidence.
Keep the first-class skill interpretation report and Skill Overview v2 contract synchronized as the report model evolves.
Use Daily SkillOps with explicit curated sources to review adaptation proposals without scanning private logs or auto-patching.
Keep the blueprint coverage report in CI so 2.0 plan drift is visible.

source_blueprint.title: Skill Overview / Skill OS 2.0 upgrade plan
reference_extensions: Adaptive self-iteration with local preference memory and approval gates
